Big Baby Eats family,

Due to an unexpected computer issue, we’re currently unable to update our online ordering link for this week’s menu.

items.title: The Flock Shop was the
items.description:
The Flock Shop was the perfect southern fried chicken experience today. The chicken was excellent & juicy. My son tried the “kinda hot” and it delivered! I had grits with bacon & Gorgonzola cheese that melted in my mouth - they were so good.  The hush puppies were hot and tasty. My husband enjoyed the chicken salad sandwich. 
If you’ve never tried pickled fried okra, now is the time to try it.

items.title: Taking grading beyond the screen. 🚧 Our first Grading Workshop brought team members from our Charlotte, Greenville, and...
items.description:
Taking grading beyond the screen. 🚧
 
Our first Grading Workshop brought team members from our Charlotte, Greenville, and Mount Pleasant offices together to connect design intent to real-world field conditions through 3D modeling exercises and hands-on demonstrations. Team members even took the lesson outside, using string lines to model grading concepts in the field and better understand how design decisions shape site conditions.
 
A great two days of collaboration, problem-solving, and knowle

items.title: The Palmetto Stop Is State Law in SC
items.description: On May 18th, 2026, South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster (R) signed into law S.812 , or the Stop-as-Yield Law for Bicycles. This makes South Carolina the 14th U.S. state to pass a version of the “Idaho Stop”, with Palmetto Walk Bike dubbing ours the “Palmetto Stop.” This is an exciting development for cycling safety in our state and enjoyed bipartisan support. items.description:
A beautiful evening spent slowing down, creating, and connecting 🌸💐
 
Our Charlotte Women’s Initiative recently enjoyed a Garden Gathering, where we created our own bouquets and enjoyed a guided meditation. It was the perfect reminder to pause, be present, and enjoy time together.
 
Thank you to @alfredwilliams_carolinas for hosting us, and to Jess from @gatherflowerstudio for sharing her creativity and helping make the evening so special.

#SWculture #SWpeople #womensinitiative

items.title: A building doesn’t appear overnight. It starts with a vision, a piece of property, and a whole lot of work that most peo...
items.description:
A building doesn’t appear overnight.

It starts with a vision, a piece of property, and a whole lot of work that most people never see.

Last week, we reached another important milestone at @christchurchanglicanmtp with the completion of the slab.

The groundwork has been laid. The foundation is in place. And soon, what has existed on paper for months will begin rising above the site for everyone to see.

These are the moments we love in construction. Watching an idea become something real.

items.title: Happy National Trails Day! 🌳 Trails shape the way people move through and experience a community, creating space for co...
items.description:
Happy National Trails Day! 🌳

Trails shape the way people move through and experience a community, creating space for connection, exploration, and everyday access to the outdoors. We’re grateful to help support spaces like these and the role they play in our communities.

What do you enjoy most when you’re on a trail? 🥾
 
#NationalTrailsDay #SWprojects #Trails #CommunityDesign

items.title: OneSpartanburg, Inc. is not affiliated with any group or funding effort attempting to influence County Council elections...
items.description: OneSpartanburg, Inc. is not affiliated with any group or funding effort attempting to influence County Council elections. Claims by candidates that we are linked to spending behind recent mailers, and that we invited candidates to meet with our investors, are false. We do aim to educate our constituents and partners on local candidates’ views on economic issues. We invited candidates to speak to municipal leaders at our regularly scheduled Mayoral meeting, and invited candidates to respond to a

items.description: The mobile on site office was delivered this week and Duke Energy was there yesterday hooking them up. The property is now completely surrounded by fencing. Construction should begin in July.   PAL will be running the trail down Liberty Street and through the apartment complex to Pine St.  They are some great houses on Liberty St.  One recently sold for $250k which is a record for the Beaumont neighborhood.

One year and ten days ago, Johnston Design Group and Edifice were hired to design and build a new stadium for Triumph SC...
items.description: One year and ten days ago, Johnston Design Group and Edifice were hired to design and build a new stadium for Triumph SC. Three weeks later, graders were already on site. And less than a year after that — we were there for the first game, the first roar of the crowd, and the first victory in their new home. It was a tall order, and it took an extraordinary team to deliver it — the City of Greenville, the Triumph organization, our partners at Edifice, and every person on the design and constructi

items.title: Early voting is winding down ahead of the June 9 Primary, with contested races for both Spartanburg County Council and S...
items.description: Early voting is winding down ahead of the June 9 Primary, with contested races for both Spartanburg County Council and SC House seats. OneSpartanburg, Inc. has sought to educate voters and our county’s business community on where candidates stand on important issues, as chambers of commerce across the country have done for hundreds of years. To learn more about where the candidates on June’s Primary ballot stand, head to https://www.onespartanburginc.com/vote.

items.title: New City/County Government Center
items.description: Steel is up to (essentially) roof level now!  We can begin to get a sense for the height/presence of the building.  Also, steel is finally going up on the north end of the building.  The parking deck has reached the central stair/elevator tower that will have pedestrian access to the interior plaza, and you can see the center car entrance on Spring too.

items.title: Every day, most of us reserve a little time for ourselves. A quiet cup of coffee. A walk. A moment to breathe. What if y...
items.description: Every day, most of us reserve a little time for ourselves. A quiet cup of coffee. A walk. A moment to breathe. What if your home did that for you — all the time? That's the idea behind Living Wellness in restorative design. Not wellness as a checklist, but as something built into the way a home is made: the quality of its light, the freshness of its air, its relationship to the land it sits on, the peace that comes from a space designed with you — your life, your rhythms, your wellbeing — in min

items.title: Rain? ✅ Mud? ✅ 24 straight hours of teamwork? ✅ From Friday night to Saturday night, our team took on the 24-Hour Relay ...
items.description:
Rain? ✅ Mud? ✅ 24 straight hours of teamwork? ✅
 
From Friday night to Saturday night, our team took on the 24-Hour Relay Race at the U.S National Whitewater Center. Between trail runs, team challenges, and thunderstorms along the way, there was never a dull moment!

#SWculture #SWpeople #relayrace

items.title: The City of Spartanburg will kick off Juneteenth Celebration Weekend with an artist talk by Spartanburg native Melvin L...
items.description: The City of Spartanburg will kick off Juneteenth Celebration Weekend with an artist talk by Spartanburg native Melvin L Nesbitt Jr., followed by a reception for his exhibition, The Get-Together: The Art of Melvin Nesbitt, Jr. Nesbitt spent much of his childhood in local public housing, an experience that continues to inspire his work today. Through vibrant mixed-media collages, he captures the joy, community, and everyday moments of Black life, offering powerful narratives that celebrate memory,

items.title: The Spartanburg area gained 400 jobs in March 2026 and has averaged approximately 255 new jobs per month over the past f...
items.description: The Spartanburg area gained 400 jobs in March 2026 and has averaged approximately 255 new jobs per month over the past five years, continuing a broader trend of workforce and economic growth across the region. Job growth impacts more than just businesses. It affects career opportunities, household stability, workforce development and long-term economic momentum across Spartanburg County. Growth conversations are also conversations about jobs, opportunity and the future of our community. Source:

We’re incredibly honored to have been awarded the Georgetown Port Redevelopment Master Plan, helping shape the future of one of the most significant waterfront redevelopment opportunities in the Lowcountry.

For us, this project is about more than redevelopment. It’s about honoring Georgetown’s history, listening to the community, and creating a waterfront destination that feels authentic to the people who call it home. Our goal is to help shape

items.title: New City/County Government Center
items.description: Good to hear about The Dan.  I've long thought we need to extend The Hopper by converting the remaining two blocks of Kennedy (between Converse and Church) to 3 lanes with a multi-use path on the north side (to match the section between Converse and Union).  This just confirms that need.  Hopefully we can get that going ASAP, so we don't have that 2 block gap for too long.  Kennedy is a state road, so it could be challenging.  But then again, they're moving quickly with East Main. Are you sure a

items.description: This is so rad! I am excited for the new Blue Moon Downtown location. Wishbone built a bike rack at the Bodega after I asked if they could get one. items.description: At Tuesday's meeting, City Council will consider a development agreement for Project Wrap.  Looks like the standard 20-year FILOT with escalating annual payments.  No workforce housing, it appears. Come out to see them and make an afternoon out of it: grab a bite at your favorite local spot, pop into a few shops, take the long way around, and enjoy downtown while everything’s in bloom. items.title: Strong Towns Spartanburg is hosting a group bike ride called Pedal Burg 🌼! Join us at Fretwell on Wednesday, June 17th ...
items.description:
Strong Towns Spartanburg is hosting a group bike ride called Pedal Burg 🌼! Join us at Fretwell on Wednesday, June 17th at 6:30 pm

We'll start the ride at Fretwell, go through the route twice, and then end back at Fretwell. The ride takes roughly 15-20 minutes and we will be going at a leasurely pace. 
Children are more than welcome to join!

The reasons we are hosting a group bike ride:
1. There is safety in numbers. Having a bunch of people biking at the same time is generally safer around car

items.title: Twenty-five years. Same mission. When JDG opened its doors in Greenville in 2001, we believed architecture could do more...
items.description: Twenty-five years. Same mission. When JDG opened its doors in Greenville in 2001, we believed architecture could do more than shelter — it could restore. Restore well-being. Restore community. Restore our relationship with the places we inhabit. That belief has never wavered. And as we step into our next chapter, we're returning to it with fresh energy and deep gratitude for the Upstate community that has grown alongside us. Here's to the next 25 — together. #JDG #RestorativeDesign #Greenville #

items.description: Last month was a super busy month! I have been focusing on The Walking Spartan Instagram while I figure out what else I want to do over here on the Substack. Last month’s back-to-back East Main Road Diet coverage and Amtrak trip to Greenville was so fun but also some of the most energy I have invested into this platform to date. I am immensely grateful for the work that I get to do and all of the great connections I have made through this journey. I figure now is a perfect time to slow down and

items.title: Welcome Kyle to our Charlotte office 🔥 As Survey Crew Chief, Kyle helps lead field operations through detailed data col...
items.description:
Welcome Kyle to our Charlotte office 🔥
 
As Survey Crew Chief, Kyle helps lead field operations through detailed data collection, property research, and equipment management. He brings hands-on surveying experience and a strong technical background, earning degrees from both Clemson University and Coastal Carolina University.
 
When he’s not in the field, you’ll probably find him trail riding dirt bikes or in the gym.
 
Excited to have you on #TeamSW, Kyle!

#SWpoeple #newhire #landsurvey #SWsur

items.title: Patio season is calling ☀️🍹 If you’re looking for a downtown spot to settle into this spring (and heading into summer),...
items.description: Patio season is calling ☀️🍹 If you’re looking for a downtown spot to settle into this spring (and heading into summer), @bluemoonbodega has the vibe covered. From colorful cocktails and refreshing bites to a beautiful outdoor dining space, it’s the kind of place you’ll want to stay all day if you could. Consider this your sign to grab a friend, order something cold, and soak up the warm weather Downtown Spartanburg 🌺🧡 #DowntownSpartanburg #PatioSeason #SpartanburgSC

items.title: A new oasis in the heart of downtown Charleston 🌿✨ American Gardens, created in partnership with Beemok Hospitality Col...
items.description:
A new oasis in the heart of downtown Charleston 🌿✨
 
American Gardens, created in partnership with Beemok Hospitality Collection, transformed a historic Meeting Street site into a vibrant gathering space designed for connection, relaxation, and community with timeless European garden-inspired charm. SeamonWhiteside helped bring the vision to life through civil engineering, landscape architecture, permitting, and site design while navigating the unique requirements of a Special Protection Area in

items.title: We're proud to share one of our most meaningful projects yet — a custom home built on the belief that great design doesn...
items.description: We're proud to share one of our most meaningful projects yet — a custom home built on the belief that great design doesn't dominate the landscape, it honors it. Watch the video to see how restorative design principles and bold architectural vision came together to create something truly special. Every decision — from siting to material selection — was made with one goal in mind: a home that feels like a natural extension of the land it lives on, not an imposition upon it. items.title: Introducing Liam 👏 Liam is a graduate of Coastal Carolina University with a degree in Sustainability and Coastal Resili...
items.description:
Introducing Liam 👏
 
Liam is a graduate of Coastal Carolina University with a degree in Sustainability and Coastal Resilience. He is a Surveying Field Technician I on our Charlotte land survey team and focuses on precision, accuracy, and producing work that meets high standards.
 
Outside of work, he enjoys fishing, playing disc golf, hiking, and exploring new places.
 
Glad to have you on the team, Liam!

#SWpeople #landsurveying
